Who They Work Closely With: Area Manager, Dept Leads, Safety, Engineering, Corporate
Why This Role Matters: Spots and manages inconsistencies in operations and create plans for smoother fulfillment across the center
Notes: Understanding facility perfomance helps create ways to adapt and do better

Who They Work Closely With: Operations Managers, Process Assistants, Warehouse Associates
Why This Role Matters: Makes sure everything in their designated zone is running smoothly and safely
Notes: Tracks daily numbers (speed and errors rates), implements direct action

Who They Work Closely With: Area Manager, Warehouse Associates (Stowers, Pickers, Packers)
Why This Role Matters: Monitors associates in real time and steps in where needed
Common Tools/Systems Used: All systems at the Warehouse Associate Level
Notes: Serves as a liasion between Area Managers and  Warehouse Associates

Main Responsibility: On-The-Job-Trainers                (Train New Hires, Oversee Workflows, Offer Support, Quality Check)
Why This Role Matters: Ensures operations run smoothly and every associate is well informed
Common Tools/Systems Used: All systems at the Warehouse Associate Level
Notes: Keeping the Ambassadors well trained, updated, and informed is crucial

Why This Role Matters: Pickers and Packers rely on this role. Accurate stowing can ensure smoother shipping
Notes: Proper stowing procedures and storage systems ensure inventory accuracy

Why This Role Matters: Ensures the correct item is picked for order processing.
Notes: Relies on stowing accuracy to ensure proper item is picked for packaging

Why This Role Matters: Ensures all items are placed and shipped with the proper order.
Notes: This position relies highly on item accuracy
